evidence: The statement asks whether the sumset A_1 + ... + A_100 of 100 cubes covers F_3^n.
The cubes live in F_3^n and the ALM91 reference concerns additive bases of vector spaces over prime fields.
The references [ALM91] 'Additive bases of vector spaces over prime fields' and [Yu25] 'Note on the Additive Basis Conjecture' frame the problem as an additive-basis question.
The problem asks for the smallest A in Z/pZ such that no element of the sumset A + A has a unique representation.
The statement asks whether X and Y must be uniformly distributed on their ranges when X + Y is uniformly distributed on its range.
indepSum is defined as the discrete convolution of two PMFs on Z, representing the sum of independent random variables.
The statement supposes A is a K-approximate group (IsApproximateSubgroup) and asks for a large S subset A with S^8 in A^4.
The hypothesis is that the open set A in [0,1] has Lebesgue measure (volume) greater than 1/3.
F(N) is defined as the largest Sidon subset of [N] and the problem asks to improve the lower bound N^{1/2} + O(1).
The problem asks to improve the upper bound N^{1/2} + 0.98183 N^{1/4} + O(1) on the largest Sidon subset of [N].
The docstring states that in the regime omega(p) ~ c p this is Szemerédi's theorem [Gr24].
The problem asks for sets A in Z/qZ of size (sqrt 2 + o(1)) q^{1/2} whose sumset A + A is all of Z/qZ.
The quantity c(p) is the infimum of the L^p norm of the self-convolution f * f of a unit-interval density.
The problem estimates the infimum of the L^p norm (eLpNorm) of the autoconvolution over unit-interval densities.
The upper-bound problem concerns the same infimum of the L^p norm of the self-convolution f * f.
The upper-bound problem asks for an improved bound on c(p), the infimal L^p norm of an autoconvolution.
